results.title: About TRACE | Trace
results.content:
# About TRACE

TRACE is a mission-driven association working to leave a positive trace on people and the planet through a circular economy where materials, products, and value chains are designed to circulate, not deplete.

We unite industry, academia, policymakers, and civil society to co-create the knowledge, collaborations, and proof of concept circular systems that make circular solutions work in practice, starting with plastics and textiles. [...] Today, more than 160 partners collaborate through TRACE to turn research and innovation into real-world change. The partnership is the foundation of everything we do. Each partner contributes knowledge, capacity, and perspective that together make it possible to build systems no single actor could create alone. Through this collaboration, TRACE translates science and innovation into tangible progress for society.

As Builder, Harmonizer, and Pathfinder, TRACE develops scalable circular systems that show how entire value chains can transform from design and production to reuse and recovery. [...] Through these efforts, TRACE helps accelerate Denmark’s transition toward a regenerative circular society by 2050, an economy that restores more than it extracts and enables people to live well within planetary boundaries.

Director Anette Juhl and Chairperson Mikkel Stenbæk Hansen introduce TRACE’s mission and partnership approach

## TRACE’s North Star Vision and mission

Our North Star is a regenerative circular society by 2050, where resources circulate in closed loops, waste is eliminated, and natural systems are restored.

This transformation supports Denmark’s targets of a 70 percent reduction in greenhouse gas emissions by 2030 and net zero by 2050, and it requires deep collaboration, systemic innovation, and a fundamental redesign of production and consumption systems.

results.title: TRACE | TRACE
results.content:
# TRACE

The TRACE database is a searchable catalogue of the full range of marine biological resources (from animals to macroalgae, protists, prokaryotes and viruses) that can be provided by the marine stations comprising the EMBRC Research Infrastructure. The catalogue includes living resources that are currently ex situ cultured (model animal and macroalgal taxa, microorganism culture collections), fixed or frozen resources maintained in sample collections, and resources that can potentially be collected from the natural environment upon demand. In the Trace database users can find information on the nature and origin of the resources of interest to them and identify the marine station(s) who can provide the biological resources via on site and/or remote access.

Home

results.title: About TRACE | TRACE
results.content:
### The University of Tennessee, Knoxville

## University Libraries

## TRACE: Tennessee Research and Creative Exchange University Libraries

## You are here

# About TRACE

# TRACE GUIDELINES

July 2017

# About TRACE

Tennessee Research and Creative Exchange (TRACE) is the University of Tennessee’s public access digital repository. Created and maintained by the University of Tennessee (UT) Libraries, TRACE is a venue and archive for Volunteers’ research and creative works. [...] TRACE contains scholarly works by UT faculty, staff, and graduate students, as well as documents from the University Archives. Content includes journal articles, technical reports, data sets, theses and dissertations, conference papers and presentations, book chapters, and other scholarly materials. These materials, and materials that are of historical value to the university, are the main focus of the collection. In addition, student work that is sponsored or approved by a faculty member, academic department, or campus unit, may be added.

TRACE helps fulfill the Libraries’ mission to provide “expertise and leadership in accessing, creating, disseminating, and preserving knowledge.”

# Benefits of TRACE [...] # Content Guidelines

The collections in TRACE are focused on the research, scholarship, creative works of UT faculty and researchers, and in some cases students, as well as materials that document the history of the university and reflect its intellectual environment.

Examples of potential content include but are not limited to:

Some content may not currently be accepted for deposit into TRACE either because it does not meet the general guidelines above or due to issues of copyright or technical limitations. For clarification of what can be deposited in TRACE, please contact trace@utk.edu.

results.title: TRACE Definition & Meaning | Dictionary.com
results.content:
# trace

## noun

a surviving mark, sign, or evidence of the former existence, influence, or action of some agent or event; vestige.

traces of an advanced civilization among the ruins.

a barely discernible indication or evidence of some quantity, quality, characteristic, expression, etc..

a trace of anger in his tone.

an extremely small amount of some chemical component.

a trace of copper in its composition.

traces, the series of footprints left by an animal.

the track left by the passage of a person, animal, or object.

the trace of her skates on the ice.

Meteorology.
precipitation of less than 0.005 inch (0.127 millimeter).

a trail or path, especially through wild or open territory, made by the passage of people, animals, or vehicles.

engram. [...] to follow the footprints, track, or traces of.

to follow, make out, or determine the course or line of, especially by going backward from the latest evidence, nearest existence, etc..

to trace one's ancestry to the Pilgrims.

to follow (footprints, evidence, the history or course of something, etc.).

to follow the course, development, or history of.

to trace a political movement.

to ascertain by investigation; find out; discover.

The police were unable to trace his whereabouts.

to draw (a line, outline, figure, etc.).

to make a plan, diagram, or map of.

to copy (a drawing, plan, etc.) by following the lines of the original on a superimposed transparent sheet.

to mark or ornament with lines, figures, etc.

to make an impression or imprinting of (a design, pattern, etc.). [...] engram.

a tracing, drawing, or sketch of something.

a lightly drawn line, as the record drawn by a self-registering instrument.

Mathematics.

the intersection of two planes, or of a plane and a surface.

the sum of the elements along the principal diagonal of a square matrix.

the geometric locus of an equation.

the visible line or lines produced on the screen of a cathode-ray tube by the deflection of the electron beam.

Linguistics.
(in generative grammar) a construct that is phonologically empty but serves to mark the place in the surface structure of a sentence from which a noun phrase has been moved by a transformational operation.

Obsolete.
a footprint.

## verb (used with object)

to follow the footprints, track, or traces of.

results.title: Trace - Athletics
results.content:
The human heart of an A.I. technology

Trace is a platform that automatically films, edits, and delivers highlights of game day footage – for families, coaches, and teams. It works by recording every angle of a game and delivering a playlist to each player, featuring their highlights. Wearable sensors track players on the field, allowing A.I. to identify key highlights — goals, great passes, tough steals — and serve them up in short, shareable clips. A personal profile makes watching, sharing, and training more engaging and efficient — without having to sift through reels and reels of footage.

Prev

Next

1/2

A lighter touch [...] Playing Better with A.I.

In a performance-driven space, Trace shifts focus to  stories of young athletes and their lasting memories. Achieving 10 million highlights in a month, our youth- centric brand’s system, featuring real user footage,  hit a jaw-dropping milestone.

We revamped Trace’s visual identity, simplifying ‘Tracebot’ and introduced a friendly lowercase wordmark for an open, approachable look. Repositioning Trace emphasizes the player journey, celebrating growth with accessible, shareable video, filling a market gap.

Our Role:

Strategy

Art Direction

Brand Identity

Brand Writing

Concept Development

UI / UX Design

Design and Development

The human heart of an A.I. technology [...] Pushing beyond performance

Competing brands told a story of performance, with darker palettes, imperative language systems, sharper edges. We designed Trace to stand in stark contrast – a nurturing, friendly, approachable presence in a landscape of adrenaline-infused brands.

Prev

Next

1/4

Celebrating connection & personal progress

results.title: Trace - Definition, Meaning & Synonyms | Vocabulary.com
results.content:
SKIP TO CONTENT

/treɪs/

IPA guide

Other forms: traces; traced; tracing

A trace of something is just a hint or suggestion of it, a very small amount left behind — like the sad cookie crumbs at the bottom of an empty cookie jar.

There are more meanings we can trace to this word trace. As a verb, trace means to follow the course or trail of something, sometimes even chasing or hunting it. It also means to draw or sketch over a picture on translucent "tracing paper." So you could say that Sherlock Holmes traced criminals throughout London, following even the smallest traces of evidence and tracing copies of the villain's notes for his files.

Definitions of trace

1. an indication that something has been present

   “there wasn't a trace of evidence for the claim” [...] type of:

   continue, go forward, proceed

   move ahead; travel onward in time or space
9. verb

   go back over again

   “trace your path”

   synonyms:  retrace

   see moresee less

   type of:

   return

   go or come back to place, condition, or activity where one has been before
10. verb

    pursue or chase relentlessly

    “The hunters traced the deer into the woods”

    synonyms:  hound, hunt

    see moresee less

    types:

    ferret

    hound or harry relentlessly

    type of:

    chase, chase after, dog, give chase, go after, tag, tail, track, trail

    go after with the intent to catch
11. noun

    a drawing created by superimposing a semitransparent sheet of paper on the original image and copying on it the lines of the original image [...] reproduce by printing

    type of:

    mark

    make or leave a mark on
13. verb

    copy by following the lines of the original drawing on a transparent sheet placed upon it; make a tracing of

    “trace a design”

    “trace a pattern”

    see moresee less

    type of:

    copy, re-create

    make a replica of
14. noun

    either of two lines that connect a horse's harness to a wagon or other vehicle or to a whiffletree

    see moresee less

    type of:

    line

    something (as a cord or rope) that is long and thin and flexible

Pronunciation

results.title: ‎Trace Teams App - App Store
results.content: Trace is the all-in-one soccer game video system. The Trace Teams iOS app is used by your team’s Trace equipment manager to control the TraceCam, update team rosters, start and stop games, and manage game uploads. Parents use the Trace Teams iOS app to capture even more angles and closeups of their player. Just select “Record MultiCam” and hit record. Your iPhone video will be saved to your camera roll and stitched to TraceCam footage to deliver epic highlights. Looking for your game footage? No app needed. It will be emailed or texted to you after each game. Recording games and editing film can be complicated, time-consuming, and expensive. Trace captures your game footage and delivers each player's highlights directly to their inboxes after each game. Players, parents, and coaches can [...] The Trace app is very simple to use and keeps the user on task with step by step instructions. The equipment set-up, viewing functions and Trace a game set-up have been very well thought out for the user. I would give the app 5 stars but have noticed one error in the roster settings. Between games one of the rosters for our club keeps adding a player to an empty Trace slot after we switch the player to a different number. We switched the player because a Trace cube is not working but when the operator opens the roster to hand out tracers it keeps putting a player in the bad slot. This does not happen on all rosters so we are in the troubleshooting phase to figure out if it is operator error or a trace app error. Once we solve this issue I would give the whole program 5 stars. The [...] the app allows on-the-field set up of the Trace video camera. it provides instructions with pictures to guide you through the process, which makes it so easy that anyone could record a game on their first try. they also include a way to get urgent ‘on-field support’ if something goes wrong, and their customer support is the best out there. a very helpful FAQ section is also accessible through the app. Trace automatically separates out several playlists which gives the option to easily watch individual player clips (the first thing we watch after filming a game are all of my daughter’s plays), or tactical moments from the game (the second thing we watch are all the goals scored, then our ‘touch chains’). you also have the option to watch the full game and the players can save and easily

results.title: Trace Teams - Apps on Google Play
results.content:
Everyone

Learn more

## About this app

Trace is the all-in-one soccer game film system. The Trace Teams app lets you control the TraceCam and the Trace Case, update team rosters, start and stop games, and manage game uploads.  
  
Recording games and editing film can be complicated, time-consuming, and expensive. Trace captures your game footage, generates tactical playlists, and delivers each player's moments directly to their inboxes after each game. Trace’s AI-powered system is the most affordable all-in-one solution for game footage, analysis, and personalized video.  
  
Use Trace Teams to:  
  
• Manage teams and update player rosters and Tracer assignments  
• Create games and manage game recordings  
• Pair your TraceCase and Camera  
• Monitor the status of your game uploads
